# Sources for App Stores, Launch Platforms, and Code Repositories

- **Apple App Store fraud analysis 2025** (Platform transparency / primary). Apple said it stopped over US$2B in potentially fraudulent transactions in 2024, removed 143M+ fraudulent ratings/reviews, 7,400+ potentially fraudulent apps from charts, and nearly 9,500 deceptive apps from search. URL: https://www.apple.com/au/newsroom/2025/05/the-app-store-prevented-more-than-9-billion-usd-in-fraudulent-transactions/
- **Google Play policy — User Ratings, Reviews, and Installs** (Platform policy / primary). Developers must not manipulate app placement by inflating ratings, reviews, or installs through fraudulent or incentivized means. URL: https://support.google.com/googleplay/android-developer/answer/9898684?hl=en
- **Product Hunt Community Guidelines** (Platform policy / primary). Prohibits mass messaging users, asking for upvotes, bots, incentivized upvotes, and other artificial activity. URL: https://help.producthunt.com/en/articles/3615694-community-guidelines
- **Product Hunt — What are points?** (Platform policy / primary). Rankings use points calculated from genuine engagement including upvotes and other signals such as comments, not raw upvote count alone. URL: https://help.producthunt.com/en/articles/10275873-what-are-points
- **GitHub Acceptable Use Policies** (Platform policy / primary). Bans spam and inauthentic activity including fake accounts, automated inauthentic activity, rank abuse such as automated starring/following, and secondary markets for inauthentic activity. URL: https://docs.github.com/en/site-policy/acceptable-use-policies/github-acceptable-use-policies
